fanInRanking.name: Reactive growth governed by a written membership/intake gate
Compose on native + agent-skills; reconstruct only the plumbing; never reconstruct orchestration
Context assembly as a budgeted injection policy — pointers over content, organized by lifetime
Hook safety — fail-open on error; deliberate blocking only via the intake gate, with kill switches
Promotion gated by an objective, binary, invoked audit (not a self-tunable score, not a session hook)
Measure success with a hook-written fallback log and a dual fallback-rate × quality metric
Session-lifecycle backbone — always-on automatic floor plus on-demand commands; no close ritual
Keep ecc installed-but-dormant as reference + backstop
Package nxtlvl as a Claude Code plugin installed via a local marketplace
Prose quality is governed harness-wide by a vendored stop-slop skill, with a condensed always-on chat convention
Quality first — size and leanness are backstops, never the objective
Pre-build a bounded confident-core of capability domains; intake gate governs the rest
Main session orchestrates; agents are scoped specialists on a lean ECC-style contract
Extend native Claude Code file-memory; build no new memory system
Agents execute, skills hold knowledge; agent definitions point to a skill as single source of truth
Authoring method — caller-agnostic skill loaded by a lean executor; eval-first disciplines, scoped
Agents that front a skill load it only when they run the skill to completion; spawn-target agents must not
Evaluation — agent-self-evaluation as advisory per-task done-condition check; formal eval suites stay reactive; gates unchanged
A global decision rule governs how decisions are made and recorded; ADR-worthy tier first
Compose, Not Reconstruct

document:ADR-011-prose-quality-stop-slop.name: Prose quality is governed harness-wide by a vendored stop-slop skill, with a condensed always-on chat convention
document:ADR-011-prose-quality-stop-slop.summary: Governs prose quality harness-wide via a vendored stop-slop skill plus a condensed always-on chat convention, rather than a hook/gate or intake-first mechanism.

document:ADR-012-agents-execute-skills-h.name: Agents execute, skills hold knowledge; agent definitions point to a skill as single source of truth
document:ADR-012-agents-execute-skills-h.summary: Separates concerns so agents execute while skills hold knowledge, with agent definitions pointing to a skill as single source of truth rather than baking rules into the prompt.

document:ADR-013-floor-on-demand-backbon.name: Session-lifecycle backbone — always-on automatic floor plus on-demand commands; no close ritual
document:ADR-013-floor-on-demand-backbon.summary: Defines the session-lifecycle backbone as an always-on automatic floor plus on-demand commands with no close ritual; supersedes ADR-008 and amends ADR-004/005/006/007.

document:ADR-014-quality-first-over-lean.name: Quality first — size and leanness are backstops, never the objective
document:ADR-014-quality-first-over-lean.summary: Reframes quality as the objective with size/leanness only as backstops; amends ADR-007 (token budget as soft ceiling) and ADR-008 (gate optimizes for quality).

document:ADR-015-agent-skill-load-rule-m.name: Agents that front a skill load it only when they run the skill to completion; spawn-target agents must not
document:ADR-015-agent-skill-load-rule-m.summary: Refines ADR-012: an agent loads its fronting skill only when it runs that skill to completion; spawn-target agents (e.g. doubt-reviewer) must not load the spawning skill.

document:ADR-016-confident-core-capabili.name: Pre-build a bounded confident-core of capability domains; intake gate governs the rest
document:ADR-016-confident-core-capabili.summary: Pre-builds a bounded confident-core of five capability domains while the intake gate governs the rest; amends ADR-008 by moving languages from reactive to build-now.

document:ADR-017-agent-design-contract.m.name: Main session orchestrates; agents are scoped specialists on a lean ECC-style contract
document:ADR-017-agent-design-contract.m.summary: Keeps the main session as orchestrator and agents as scoped specialists on a lean ECC-style contract, rejecting a per-language/per-domain agent matrix and deferring per-agent model pinning.

document:ADR-018-agent-authoring-method..name: Authoring method — caller-agnostic skill loaded by a lean executor; eval-first disciplines, scoped
document:ADR-018-agent-authoring-method..summary: Sets the agent authoring method: a caller-agnostic skill loaded by a lean executor, with eval-first disciplines scoped rather than reconstructing review/dev substance or adopting ECC internals wholesale.

document:ADR-019-agent-evaluation-model..name: Evaluation — agent-self-evaluation as advisory per-task done-condition check; formal eval suites stay reactive; gates unchanged
document:ADR-019-agent-evaluation-model..summary: Adopts agent-self-evaluation as an advisory per-task done-condition check, keeps formal eval suites reactive, and leaves promotion gates unchanged.

document:ADR-020-agent-debugging-model.m.name: Debugging — adopt the introspection self-debug loop; scope the architecture audit to owned layers; diagnostic, never a gate
document:ADR-020-agent-debugging-model.m.summary: Adopts an introspection self-debug loop and scopes the architecture audit to owned layers, keeping it diagnostic and never a gate.

document:ADR-001-plugin-local-marketplac.name: Package nxtlvl as a Claude Code plugin installed via a local marketplace
document:ADR-001-plugin-local-marketplac.summary: Decides nxtlvl ships as a Claude Code plugin installed through a local marketplace rather than hand-edited config, a sync script, or a loose skill collection — making install/uninstall atomic and reproducible.

document:ADR-002-ecc-dormant-reference-b.name: Keep ecc installed-but-dormant as reference + backstop
document:ADR-002-ecc-dormant-reference-b.summary: Keeps the ecc plugin installed but dormant (hooks neutralized) so it serves as a reference harness and fallback backstop instead of being uninstalled or left active.

document:ADR-003-compose-not-reconstruct.name: Compose on native + agent-skills; reconstruct only the plumbing; never reconstruct orchestration
document:ADR-003-compose-not-reconstruct.summary: Establishes the founding three-tier build rule — compose on native + agent-skills, reconstruct only plumbing, never reconstruct orchestration. Superseded by ADR-035 and amended by ADR-027.

document:ADR-004-extend-native-memory.md.name: Extend native Claude Code file-memory; build no new memory system
document:ADR-004-extend-native-memory.md.summary: Extends native Claude Code file-memory and builds no new store, with later amendments adding a separate nxtlvl instinct store and provenance as the ownership boundary.

document:ADR-005-fallback-log-dual-metri.name: Measure success with a hook-written fallback log and a dual fallback-rate × quality metric
document:ADR-005-fallback-log-dual-metri.summary: Measures harness success via a hook-written fallback log and a dual fallback-rate × quality metric; the session-level manual quality score was later retired in favor of instinct-confidence distribution.

document:ADR-006-hook-fail-open-gated-bl.name: Hook safety — fail-open on error; deliberate blocking only via the intake gate, with kill switches
document:ADR-006-hook-fail-open-gated-bl.summary: Mandates absolute fail-open on hook error, permitting deliberate blocking only through the intake gate and always with kill switches; a 2026-06-19 clarifying note adds a carve-out without reversing the rule.

document:ADR-007-context-budgeted-inject.name: Context assembly as a budgeted injection policy — pointers over content, organized by lifetime
document:ADR-007-context-budgeted-inject.summary: Treats context assembly as a budgeted injection policy favoring pointers over content and organized by lifetime; amended by ADR-014 (quality-gated recall) and ADR-013.

document:ADR-008-reactive-growth-intake-.name: Reactive growth governed by a written membership/intake gate
document:ADR-008-reactive-growth-intake-.summary: Governs harness growth reactively through a written membership/intake gate. Superseded by ADR-013 and amended by ADR-016 (confident-core) and ADR-014 (quality-first).

document:ADR-009-objective-invoked-audit.name: Promotion gated by an objective, binary, invoked audit (not a self-tunable score, not a session hook)
document:ADR-009-objective-invoked-audit.summary: Gates promotion behind an objective, binary, explicitly-invoked audit rather than a self-tunable score or continuous session hook; concrete rubric deferred to Phase ≥1.

document:ADR-010-global-decision-rule.md.name: A global decision rule governs how decisions are made and recorded; ADR-worthy tier first
document:ADR-010-global-decision-rule.md.summary: Establishes a global decision rule composing the interview→spec→plan→doc pipeline and an ADR-worthy threshold, layered global-default with per-project override.

concept:compose-not-reconstruct.name: Compose, Not Reconstruct
concept:compose-not-reconstruct.summary: The founding build doctrine that nxtlvl reconstructs only the plumbing and composes workflow substance from reviewed harnesses, leaving orchestration native — learn-by-reconstruction applies to the harness, not the SDLC content.

concept:fail-open.name: fail-open
concept:fail-open.summary: The recurring safety stance that hooks and gates fail open on error, with deliberate blocking confined to invoked gates plus kill switches.

concept:objective-gate.name: Objective Gate (block on facts, never on taste)
concept:objective-gate.summary: The discipline that promotion/graduation gates block only on objective, binary criteria (integrity, declared evals, justification) and never on taste or quality, with crashes failing open and deliberate blocks signalled by exit 2.

concept:agents-execute-skills-hold-knowl.name: agents-execute-skills-hold-knowledge
concept:agents-execute-skills-hold-knowl.summary: The recurring separation where agents execute and skills hold knowledge, agent definitions pointing to a skill as single source of truth.

concept:quality-first.name: quality-first
concept:quality-first.summary: The recurring stance that quality is the objective and size/leanness are backstops, governing context budgets, growth, and prose.

concept:reactive-growth-intake-gate.name: reactive-growth-intake-gate
concept:reactive-growth-intake-gate.summary: The recurring governance model where the harness grows reactively through a written membership/intake gate rather than proactive breadth.

document:ADR-021-agent-orchestration-mod.name: ADR-021: Agent Orchestration Model
document:ADR-021-agent-orchestration-mod.summary: Adopts a gated delegate-don't-inline orchestration pipeline where nxtlvl owns the composition but never the dispatch runtime, with a scoped agent roster. Rejects pre-building the full orchestration command family up front.

document:ADR-022-agent-operation-model.m.name: ADR-022: Agent Operation Model
document:ADR-022-agent-operation-model.m.summary: Establishes that nxtlvl runs as gated, interactive, single-operator sessions with an automatic observation floor, rejecting autonomous, continuous, and enterprise runtimes. Reuses existing metric, gate, and kill switches rather than new operational machinery.

document:ADR-023-github-workflow-skill-a.name: ADR-023: GitHub Workflow Skill and Conventions
document:ADR-023-github-workflow-skill-a.summary: Standardizes the GitHub workflow as a vendored skill that composes the review agent, adopting Conventional Commits with no attribution. Superseded by ADR-024, which re-ships the workflow as a three-layer domain.

document:ADR-024-git-workflows-domain-co.name: ADR-024: git-workflows Domain (command -> agent -> skill)
document:ADR-024-git-workflows-domain-co.summary: Ships the GitHub workflow as a three-layer git-workflows domain (command -> agent -> skill) with an isolated, edit-less executor that can commit and push but never rewrite source. Supersedes ADR-023's single in-context skill shape.

document:ADR-025-project-identity-observ.name: ADR-025: Project Identity & Observer Concurrency
document:ADR-025-project-identity-observ.summary: Defines project identity as the git common directory and protects observer state with atomic writes (tmp + rename) plus a per-session single-flight guard. Rejects folder-path identity and ecc's PID/lock/daemon machinery.

document:ADR-026-ideation-domain-orchest.name: ADR-026: Ideation Domain (orchestrator skill + isolated agents)
document:ADR-026-ideation-domain-orchest.summary: Ships the ideation phase as a three-layer domain with a main-thread orchestrator skill plus isolated read-only agents, inverting ADR-024's isolated-executor shape because the interview must stay interactive on the main thread.

document:ADR-027-router-endorses-only-es.name: ADR-027: Router Endorses Only Established Items
document:ADR-027-router-endorses-only-es.summary: The nxtlvl router endorses only established nxtlvl items and delists the agent-skills fallthrough floor, collapsing precedence to nxtlvl -> native and going dark at unowned phases. Amends ADR-003's compose-on-native-plus-agent-skills strategy.

document:ADR-028-project-management-doma.name: ADR-028: Project Management Domain (manage-and-see)
document:ADR-028-project-management-doma.summary: Ships a Project Management workflow domain as a manage-and-see layer with a standalone interactive dashboard over a single-writer state library, delegating execution to domain agents. Classified as a process domain so it does not trip ADR-016's capability-domain brake.

document:ADR-029-deepwiki-orientation-no.name: ADR-029: DeepWiki Orients but Never Testifies
document:ADR-029-deepwiki-orientation-no.summary: DeepWiki accelerates harness-review Phase 2 as an orientation source only — leads, not evidence — with every claim verified at source, a scout read-only by withheld tools, zero DeepWiki citations in artifacts, and silent degradation for local/private repos.

document:ADR-030-context7-testifies-prim.name: ADR-030: Context7 Testifies (primary sources, version-pinned)
document:ADR-030-context7-testifies-prim.summary: Context7 produces evidence rather than mere leads (the inverse of ADR-029): every claim is cite-stamped with an official doc URL and resolved version, never Context7 itself, served by a scout read-only by withheld tools with graceful degradation and bounded spend.

document:ADR-031-labs-in-sandbox-topolog.name: ADR-031: Labs in Sandbox Topology
document:ADR-031-labs-in-sandbox-topolog.summary: harness-lab and evals-lab live as tracked subdirs under Developer/sandbox/nxtlvl-labs/ — not separate repos, not gitignored scratch — where graduation is an in-repo git mv. Records the 2026-06-22 relocation away from the original separate-repo design.

document:ADR-032-cells-installable-as-pl.name: ADR-032: Cells Installable as Plugin Architecture
document:ADR-032-cells-installable-as-pl.summary: Capability cells use stage-as-data manifests (a stage: field changes; the cell never moves directories, avoiding files-as-state) and the lab is installable as a CC plugin for high-fidelity dogfooding in a scratch profile.

document:ADR-033-three-part-objective-gr.name: ADR-033: Three-Part Objective Graduation Contract
document:ADR-033-three-part-objective-gr.summary: A cell graduates only when all three objective criteria pass — integrity, declared evals meeting pre-committed graduation_criteria, and intake justification present — with taste/quality warning only, crash failing open (exit 0), and deliberate block at exit 2. Honors ADR-009's objective gate and ADR-006's fail-open rule.

document:ADR-034-typescript-default-nati.name: ADR-034: TypeScript Default via Native Type-Stripping
document:ADR-034-typescript-default-nati.summary: TypeScript becomes the default harness language (no new JavaScript), run via native Node type-stripping with no build step, erasable-syntax-only, and a typecheck-only tsconfig; other languages stay first-class when justified and capability domains are untouched.

document:ADR-035-compose-substance-defer.name: ADR-035: Compose Substance, Defer Own Orchestration
document:ADR-035-compose-substance-defer.summary: Reconstruct only the plumbing and compose the workflow substance from any reviewed harness (agent-skills is one source among many, no privileged floor), orchestrating on native through the build-out with nxtlvl's own runtime a deferred exploration. Supersedes ADR-003 and generalizes ADR-027.

document:README.md.name: ADR Index (README)
document:README.md.summary: Index table for all 35 nxtlvl Architecture Decision Records, listing each ADR's decision summary and status (including supersede/amend relationships) and pointing at the intent/spec/plan anchors the ADRs consume.

concept:three-layer-domain.name: Three-Layer Domain (command -> agent -> skill)
concept:three-layer-domain.summary: The recurring nxtlvl domain shape where a command fronts an isolated executor agent that loads a single-source-of-truth skill, with variations such as a main-thread orchestrator skill when interactivity is required.

concept:read-only-by-withheld-tools.name: Read-Only by Withheld Tools
concept:read-only-by-withheld-tools.summary: The pattern of enforcing a scout's read-only discipline structurally — by withholding write tools from its tool grant — rather than relying on prompt instructions alone.
